questions.question: What is the single most important principle for high availability on AWS?

answers.text: Use the largest available instance type
Keep everything in one Availability Zone for low latency

questions.feedback: Spreading across multiple AZs removes the single-data-centre point of failure, the core HA principle.

questions.question: A web tier hands off slow background jobs so it can respond immediately, and workers process them when ready. Which service fits best?
questions.feedback: SQS buffers work in a queue so producers and consumers scale independently and spikes don't overwhelm the workers.

questions.question: A workload must recover within minutes with almost no data loss, and budget is not a concern. Which DR strategy fits?
questions.feedback: The lowest RTO/RPO comes from running full production in more than one Region (multi-site), at the highest cost.

questions.question: You need one published message to be delivered to several independent subscribers at once. Which service?
questions.feedback: SNS is publish/subscribe: publish once to a topic and it is pushed to all subscribers (a fan-out pattern).

questions.question: Which are true about RTO and RPO?

answers.text: RTO is how long you can be down before recovery
RPO is how much data (measured in time) you can afford to lose
A lower RTO generally costs more to achieve
RPO and durability are the same thing

questions.feedback: RTO = downtime tolerance, RPO = data-loss tolerance, and tighter targets cost more. Durability (data survival) is a separate concept.
